articles.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
articles.summary: Tech companies are rapidly expanding AI data centers as the physical foundation for AI development, but this expansion has sparked global conflicts over power consumption, environmental impact, and effects on local communities.

articles.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
articles.summary: Sony views AI as a powerful tool for game development while emphasizing that human vision, design, and emotional impact remain central to PlayStation games.

articles.title: Microsoft was worried OpenAI would run off to Amazon and ‘shit-talk’ Azure
articles.summary: Court documents from the Musk v. Altman trial reveal Microsoft executives feared OpenAI might leave for Amazon and criticize Azure. The concerns emerged during early partnership discussions in 2017.

articles.title: Voice AI in India is hard. Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway.
articles.summary: Wispr Flow accelerated growth in India following its Hinglish language rollout, despite ongoing challenges faced by voice AI products in the region.

articles.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
articles.summary: AI's rapid growth has introduced numerous new terms and slang that can confuse people. This glossary clarifies definitions of important AI vocabulary and phrases commonly encountered.

articles.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
articles.summary: Nvidia has committed $40 billion to equity AI deals in 2024, demonstrating its significant investment in the broader AI ecosystem beyond its core business.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS. The funding capitalizes on developer demand for simpler alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, a free open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges to recruit engineers. The innovative marketing stunt decoded hidden puzzles that attracted thousands of applicants.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. Instructure restored service Friday after identifying unauthorized activity; user data including names and emails were compromised.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detector Mythos found 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives. The breakthrough demonstrates AI-assisted vulnerability detection's potential in software security.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal usage has surged despite predictions of graphical interfaces replacing command lines. The CLI remains superior for many tasks, offering efficiency beyond point-and-click interfaces.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
articles.summary: In week two of Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, the company fired back claiming Musk pushed for a for-profit structure and is suing due to losing control battles, not broken promises about nonprofit status.

articles.title: Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: Eight cruise ship passengers contracted hantavirus, a rare rat-transmitted virus with three fatalities. The ship will dock safely in the Canary Islands as health experts don't expect a pandemic similar to COVID-19.

articles.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
articles.summary: AI is rapidly spreading throughout society, yet uncertainty remains about its true impact on jobs, the economy, and daily life. Society faces discomfort and ambiguity regarding whether AI adoption is excessive or insufficient.

articles.title: "OncoAgent: A Dual-Tier Multi-Agent Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oncology Clinical Decision Support"
articles.summary: OncoAgent is a dual-tier multi-agent AI framework designed for oncology clinical decision support while preserving patient privacy. It enables secure, intelligent medical recommendations for cancer care without compromising sensitive data.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a mixture of experts pretraining approach that enables emergent modularity in AI models. This technique improves model specialization and efficiency by allowing different experts to handle distinct tasks.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM advances from V0 to V1 with focus on correctness in reinforcement learning. The update prioritizes accurate model behavior before implementing corrections and optimizations.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI development and its potential impact. The conversation explores emerging trends and next steps in artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation. These AI solutions help companies generate imaginative content more efficiently.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to developers. The initiative aims to advance sustainable farming practices through AI and technology.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, partnering advertising legends with local businesses to create innovative AI-powered ads. The initiative showcases how creative professionals can leverage AI to help small companies reach wider audiences.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google offers five gardening tips using AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features to help plants thrive. Users can leverage these tools for plant care guidance and product shopping.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners to launch the Future Vision film competition with a $3.5 million prize pool.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve's Gemini-powered coding agent scales impact across business, infrastructure, and science domains. The algorithms drive innovation through advanced AI-assisted development.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This innovation explores how artificial intelligence can work alongside medical professionals to enhance patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company stole a charity. Evidence revealed Musk may have used OpenAI models to train xAI.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with improved coding capabilities and safety monitoring, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's new ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text generation, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6 updates from the previous week.

articles.title: RingCentral adds Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to AI Receptionist
articles.summary: RingCentral expanded its AI Receptionist product with integrations for Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp, enabling it to handle orders, appointments, and customer messages across multiple channels for small to mid-sized businesses.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is being deployed in the UK NHS to alleviate strain on hospitals and reduce the 7.25 million patient waiting list by managing care in community settings.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses leveraging enterprise data through AI, addressing challenges in data management and the choice between cloud and local compute solutions for AI models.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture that improves autonomous robot navigation by addressing localization, self-localization, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables LLMs to handle 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding context window capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are unintentionally publishing sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers through a supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: NVIDIA AI Just Released cuda-oxide: An Experimental Rust-to-CUDA Compiler Backend that Compiles SIMT GPU Kernels Directly to PTX
articles.summary: NVIDIA released cuda-oxide, an experimental Rust-to-CUDA compiler that enables developers to write GPU kernels directly in Rust, compiling to PTX without requiring C/C++ or domain-specific languages.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Recover Hidden Malware IOCs with FLARE-FLOSS Beyond Classic Strings Analysis
articles.summary: FLARE-FLOSS is a malware analysis tool that recovers hidden and obfuscated strings from Windows PE files using static analysis and emulation, uncovering indicators of compromise that traditional string utilities miss.

articles.title: NVIDIA AI Releases Star Elastic: One Checkpoint that Contains 30B, 23B, and 12B Reasoning Models with Zero-Shot Slicing
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Star Elastic embeds multiple nested submodels within a single parent reasoning model, eliminating the need for separate training runs for different model sizes. This reduces compute costs and storage requirements for teams deploying multiple LLM variants.

articles.title: I measured 5G signals of AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon in a small town - here's what the data says
articles.summary: A tester measures 5G signal coverage from AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon in small towns using Samsung phones, expanding testing beyond cities and highways.

articles.title: How I customized my Sonos speaker to make my home theater sound truly immersive
articles.summary: Sonos speakers offer customizable audio settings that enhance home theater sound quality. Users missing out on these adjustments can significantly improve their listening experience.

articles.title: I use these 10 secret Netflix codes to find hidden movies - here's how to enter them
articles.summary: Netflix offers secret codes to unlock hidden movies in buried genres and micro-categories at no extra cost. Users can access these exclusive collections by entering specific codes.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: AI agents now implement permission-gated tool calling in Python, enabling controlled access to functions and resources. This advancement moves agents beyond passive chatbots toward more secure, autonomous systems.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses strategies for mastering tool calling in AI agents, providing a roadmap for implementation. It covers techniques to enable agents to effectively use external tools for enhanced capabilities.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses implementing statistical guardrails for non-deterministic agents, which produce varying outputs from identical inputs across multiple runs.

articles.title: LLM Summarizers Skip the Identification Step
articles.summary: LLM summarizers fail by skipping the identification step, similar to regressions that don't validate data support. This methodological gap undermines their reliability.

articles.title: The Must-Know Topics for an LLM Engineer
articles.summary: Essential topics for LLM engineers span from tokenization to evaluation, covering how modern language models work in practice and their implementation.

articles.title: RAG Is Blind to Time — I Built a Temporal Layer to Fix It in Production
articles.summary: RAG systems lack temporal awareness, retrieving outdated information instead of current facts. The author built a temporal layer that filters expired data and prioritizes time-sensitive information.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue American AI leadership requires energy leadership. They emphasize affordable energy's crucial role in enabling societal opportunities and technological advancement.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin single sign-on, enabling faster logins for cloud gaming. Seven new games have been added to the platform, improving instant access across devices.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric emerges as the leading AI networking technology, deployed by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle for building powerful AI infrastructure at gigascale.

articles.title: Halliburton enhances seismic workflow creation with Amazon Bedrock and Generative AI
articles.summary: Halliburton partnered with AWS to develop an AI-powered assistant using Amazon Bedrock that simplifies seismic workflow creation, reducing manual configuration of 100+ specialized tools and improving accessibility for energy exploration.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS introduces EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML to address GPU shortage, enabling companies to secure reliable short-term GPU capacity for machine learning training and inference workloads.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: This article discusses implementing reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to improve LLM training by creating reliable feedback signals. The approach uses verification and transparency to enhance model performance in tasks like mathematical reasoning and code generation.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Research improves Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce executable shell commands for file operations and system tasks.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo introduces streaming tokens and tools support for multi-turn agentic interactions, enabling assistants to interleave reasoning with tool calls while maintaining structured conversation flow.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks for exascale performance, requiring new scheduling approaches like Slurm block scheduling to optimize system and workload efficiency.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring their distinct attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional experience developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse backgrounds can drive breakthrough advancements in cutting-edge tech fields.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks into parallel subtasks, determine concurrent thread counts, and coordinate execution based on problem complexity for more efficient inference scaling.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, integrated stochasticity for exploration, and improved gradient shaping for visual models.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, advancing interpretability research to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.
